data.body:
EXPERIMENT 2 — Gerrit webhook->diff gap (d251 S4a/S4b). Ran a REAL read-only experiment against the live public Gerrit gerrit-review.googlesource.com (no docker needed). PROVEN:
(a) Change-metadata JSON (GET /changes/?q=...&o=CURRENT_REVISION) is XSSI-guarded with the )]}' prefix and carries NO diff/patch field (HAS_DIFF_FIELD: False) — the webhooks-plugin payload is an EVENT (patchset-created metadata), strictly less than this, so the diff is NEVER in the webhook body.
(b) GET /changes/{n}/revisions/current/patch returns a BASE64 blob (first chars 'RnJvbSBi...'), which base64-decodes to a git format-patch ('From <sha> Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001 / Subject: [PATCH] ...'). So the receiver MUST make a separate authenticated REST call to obtain the diff; it must base64-decode the result.
(c) GET /changes/{n}/revisions/current/files/ returns JSON (XSSI-guarded) — the structured per-file alternative.
IMPLICATION: d251 S4b (918c) already has Round-9 AC ('fetches diff via Gerrit REST as diff_text=') and Round-10 AC ('invokes review_code() with a valid repo context / fetched checkout at the change ref'). The GAP the review caught is that the EPIC body's Round-9 seam still types the adapter as code_review_decision(diff_text)->{decision} (diff_text ONLY), contradicting S4b's Round-10 reality (needs diff_text + repo_root + ref). REMEDY: reconcile the epic seam contract to code_review_decision(diff_text, repo_root, ref)->{decision: PASS|BLOCK}, give it a concrete name+module, co-locate the error/fallback rule with the schema, and add an explicit covering AC; b744-WS6 (4119) must implement the SAME signature. Also: provision the bot's OUTBOUND read credential (Gerrit HTTP token works for REST /a/ AND git-over-HTTPS clone of the change ref) in S1 SSM scaffolding + S4a.

EXPERIMENT 3 — discriminates_novelty bar (7d43 BLOCK). The eval scorers (eval_scorers.py:212-257) are PER-CASE booleans at a 0.5 midpoint: _discriminates_true_from_false uses v>=0.5; _discriminates_impact_levels uses imp>=_IMPACT_MAJOR_FLOOR(0.5). The spec (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml) gates at the SPEC level: gate: at_least(2) over epochs:3 + coverage_threshold:0.8; deterministic discriminators carry NO per-scorer threshold field (only the llm-judge scorer has threshold:0.7, gates:false). So THREE distinct numbers were being conflated: (1) RUNTIME drop threshold T_novel=0.7 (Pass-3: novelty<0.7 => treat as carryover/floored) — house precision-first default, config-overridable; (2) the eval per-case discrimination floor = 0.5 midpoint (mirror the two sibling discriminators); (3) the eval BAR = spec gate at_least(2)/epochs:3 + coverage_threshold:0.8 over a labeled novel/carryover pair set. The BLOCK is about #3 (and its relationship to #2). RESOLUTION recorded into 7d43 + child 150b.
